title: Processing large query results in database accelerator environment
abstract: A computer-implemented method for facilitating large data transfers from a first data management system (212) to a second data management system (202) is disclosed. The method comprises receiving data from the first data management system (212) by a first buffer component (708), rerouting, upon the first buffer component (708) reaching a predefined fill-level, dynamically the received data to a second buffer components (710), wherein the second buffer components (710) is adapted to process the rerouted received data, forwarding, by the second buffer components (710), the rerouted data once the first buffer component (708) is again ready for receiving the rerouted data from the second buffer components (710), and sending, by a sending components (712), the data buffered in the first buffer component (708) to the second data management system (202).
claims:
Claims 1. A computer-implemented method for facilitating large data transfers from a first data management system to a second data management system, said method comprising - receiving data from said first data management system by a first buffer component, - rerouting, upon said first buffer component reaching a predefined fill-level, dynamically said received data to a second buffer component, wherein said second buffer component is adapted to process said rerouted received data, - forwarding, by said second buffer component, said rerouted data once said first buffer component is again ready for receiving said rerouted data from said second buffer component, and - sending, by a sending component, said data buffered in said first buffer component to said second data management system. 2. The method according to the preceding claim, wherein said first data management system is optimized for analytical workload. 3. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein said second data management system is adapted for receiving queries from a software application. 4. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein said processing of said rerouted received data by said second buffer component comprises at least one activity selected out of a group consisting of compressing said rerouted received data, storing said compressed rerouted received data, and decompressing said rerouted received data. 5. The method according to the preceding claim, wherein said compressing, storing, and decompressing said rerouted received data is performed by one or more processing threads in an interleaved manner for packets of said rerouted received data. 6. The method according to the preceding claim, wherein said one or more processing threads of said second buffer component are idle while not receiving any rerouted received data from said first buffer component. 7. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein said first buffer component is adapted to convert said received data according to predefined requirements of said second data management. 8. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein said second data management system is a row-oriented relational database system. 9. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein said first data management system is a column-oriented database management system. 10. The method according to any of the preceding claims, also comprising - determining a data rate of data being transferred out of said first buffer component for a determination whether said first buffer component is again ready for receiving said rerouted data from said second buffer component. 11. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein a sequence of data being transferred out of said first buffer component is compliant with a query having triggered said receiving data from said first data management system by said first buffer component. 12. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein said second buffer component comprises a data path comprising at least one component out of said group comprising a compression buffer, a compressor thread, a spilling file write buffer, a spilling file writer thread, a spilling file queue, a spilling file reader thread, a decompression buffer, and a decompressor thread. 13. A data transfer system for facilitating large data transfers from a first data management system to a second data management system, said system comprising - a processor and a memory communicatively coupled to said processor, wherein said 

 memory stores program code portions that, when executed, enable said processor to - receive data from said first data management system by a first buffer component, - reroute, upon said first buffer component reaching a predefined fill-level, dynamically said received data to a second buffer component, wherein said second buffer component is adapted to process said rerouted received data, - forward, by said second buffer component, said rerouted data once said first buffer component is again ready for receiving said rerouted data from said second buffer component, and - send, by a sending component, said data buffered in said first buffer component to said second data management system. 14. The system according to the preceding claim, wherein said first data management system is optimized for analytical workload. 15. The system according to any of the two preceding claims, wherein said second data management system is adapted for receiving queries from a software application. 16. The system according to any of the three preceding claims, wherein said processing of said rerouted received data by said second buffer component comprises at least one activity selected out of a group consisting of compressing said rerouted received data, storing said compressed rerouted received data, and decompressing said rerouted received data. 17. The system according to the preceding claim, wherein said compressing, storing and decompressing said rerouted received data is performed by one or more processing threads in an interleaved manner for packets of said rerouted received data. 18. The system according to the preceding claim, wherein said one or more processing threads of said second buffer component are idle while not receiving any rerouted 

 received data from said first buffer component. 19. The system according to any of the six preceding claims, wherein said first buffer component is adapted to convert said received data according to predefined requirements of said second data management system. 20. The system according to any of the seven preceding claims, wherein said second data management system is a row-oriented relational database system. 21. The system according to any of the eight preceding claims, wherein said first data management system is a column-oriented database management system. 22. The system according to any of the nine preceding claims, wherein said processor, when executing said program code, is also enabled to - determine a data rate of data being transferred out of said first buffer component for a determination whether said first buffer component is again ready for receiving said rerouted data from said second buffer component. 23. The system according to any of the ten preceding claims, wherein a sequence of data being transferred out of said first buffer component is compliant with a query having triggered said receiving data from said first data management system by said first buffer component. 24. The system according to any of the eleven preceding claims, wherein said second buffer component comprises a data path comprising at least one component out of said group comprising a compression buffer, a compressor thread, a spilling file write buffer, a spilling file writer thread, a spilling file queue, a spilling file reader thread, a decompression buffer, and a decompressor thread. 25. A computer program product for facilitating large data transfers from a first data management system to a second data management system, said computer program product comprising a computer readable storage medium having program instructions embodied therewith, said program instructions being executable by one or more computing systems or controllers to cause said one or more computing systems to 

- receive data from said first data management system by a first buffer component, - reroute, upon said first buffer component reaching a predefined fill-level, dynamically said received data to a second buffer component, wherein said second buffer component is adapted to process said rerouted received data, - forward, by said second buffer component, said rerouted data once said first buffer component is again ready for receiving said rerouted data from said second buffer component, and - send, by a sending component, said data buffered in said first buffer component to said second data management system.
